title: Lost Verizon

jokes.text: Teacher's diet is working.
jokes.setup: Chalkboard gag referencing a teacher's diet
jokes.punchline: The ironic observation that the diet is 'working'
jokes.explanation: Standard chalkboard gag with minimal setup. Economical but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Out of gas?! But how? I put a dollar in this morning and we've only driven 90 cents.
jokes.setup: Car running out of gas
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd logic treating gas like exact change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treats gas purchases like exact change. Great character integration and absurd but internally consistent reasoning.

jokes.text: You had to drive with the windows down, didn't ya, Rockefeller?
jokes.setup: Accusatory tone about wasteful driving
jokes.punchline: Calling someone 'Rockefeller' over rolling down windows
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer penny-pinching with over-the-top nickname. Good character consistency and sarcasm.

jokes.text: Let's see, B-12...
jokes.setup: Reading bingo strategy guide seriously
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of having a strategy guide for pure chance
jokes.explanation: Good Grampa character moment - treating random chance like it requires study. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This counts as a parent-teacher conference!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably in some inappropriate situation at school
jokes.punchline: Declaring any interaction with school staff counts as the required conference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer rationalization - finding loopholes to avoid responsibility. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Principal crossing Freewy - Expect delays
jokes.setup: Traffic sign format
jokes.punchline: Treating Principal Skinner like a road hazard with misspelled 'freeway'
jokes.explanation: Clever visual pun treating a person like traffic infrastructure. Great economy and structure, bonus for misspelling.

jokes.text: I couldn't get a hold of him. No cell phone. But if I know Bart, he's busy doing something awesome.
jokes.setup: Building up Bart as doing something cool
jokes.punchline: Cut to Bart having a tea party
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup. Builds expectation of cool activity, delivers wholesome tea party. Good contrast.

jokes.text: Because you love the taste of my imaginary tea.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning why he agreed to tea party
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact response about imaginary tea
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa deadpan delivery. Treating imaginary tea as having actual taste qualities. Sweet sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: Hey, Spaz! While you were here spazzing yourself, you totally missed out.
jokes.setup: Nelson addressing Bart mockingly
jokes.punchline: Using 'spaz' as both noun and verb in same sentence
jokes.explanation: Typical Nelson bullying with mild wordplay. Functional character moment but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What, do you think I'm crazy?
jokes.setup: Lisa offering imaginary cell phone
jokes.punchline: Bart rejecting imaginary solution while accepting imaginary tea
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bart's inconsistent logic about imaginary items. Nice character contradiction.

jokes.text: Your son is lamer than Milhouse. What does that say about you?
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about not having a cell phone
jokes.punchline: Using Milhouse as the ultimate measure of lameness, then turning it on Marge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - not only is Bart lame, he's lamer than Milhouse, and what does that say about Marge? Perfect logical progression of insult.

jokes.text: As it is, I'm buying frozen peas on installments.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they can't afford a cell phone
jokes.punchline: Family so poor they need payment plans for frozen vegetables
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of poverty - installment plans on frozen peas is perfectly absurd but believable for the Simpsons. Great economy.

jokes.text: And I'm never gonna go to Machu Picchu. In this family, you get used to disappointments.
jokes.setup: Bart lamenting about cell phone
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact acceptance of family disappointment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family dysfunction delivered with Lisa's characteristic resignation. Good character moment and sets up Machu Picchu plot.

jokes.text: Don't tell me where I go. A dollar? What for? It's the least I can do. These balls cost five dollars new.
jokes.setup: Bart returning golf ball expecting reward
jokes.punchline: Rich person's condescending 'generosity' with terrible math
jokes.explanation: Good class commentary - rich person's 'generous' dollar for five-dollar ball. Solid setup for Bart's scheme.

jokes.text: This is why my kids go to private school.
jokes.setup: Bart doing basic math about balls needed
jokes.punchline: Rich person's elitist response to simple arithmetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect rich person snobbery - dismissing basic math as reason for private school. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Weekends and summers, I'm Greenskeeper Willie.
jokes.setup: Willie appearing at golf course
jokes.punchline: Groundskeeper Willie becoming Greenskeeper Willie with seasonal job
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on Willie's job title. Makes perfect sense for his character to have seasonal work. Good economy.

jokes.text: Now I won't have to pleasure a country-club wife to get me steak dinner.
jokes.setup: Willie celebrating finding golf balls
jokes.punchline: Revealing his previous desperate means of getting food
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Willie revelation. Dark humor that's completely in character - his desperate poverty leading to questionable arrangements.

jokes.text: Hey, Leary, if you want to have a tea party, go back to Boston.
jokes.setup: Denis Leary presumably playing poorly
jokes.punchline: Boston Tea Party reference combined with emasculating 'tea party'
jokes.explanation: Decent historical pun connecting Boston Tea Party to Denis Leary's Boston origins and poor golf performance.

jokes.text: "Ice Age" was the name of the film. I played Diego, the vicious saber-toothed tiger, who, guess what, has a heart.
jokes.setup: Someone calling him 'Ice Age'
jokes.punchline: Denis Leary's defensive, detailed explanation of his animated role
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity self-parody - Denis Leary being overly defensive about animated character. Meta humor about voice acting career.

jokes.text: You can even watch commercials on it.
jokes.setup: Bart excited about found cell phone features
jokes.punchline: Treating commercials as a selling point rather than annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on early smartphone features and Bart's excitement over anything new. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I'm not supposed to look out the window when I'm alone.
jokes.setup: Bart asking Milhouse to look out window
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's oddly specific parental restriction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse overprotected childhood detail. Bizarre but believable restriction that raises disturbing questions.

jokes.text: You want Denis Leary? Wrong number, dumb-ass.
jokes.setup: Phone call coming in on stolen phone
jokes.punchline: Bart rudely dismissing legitimate call for phone's owner
jokes.explanation: Good ironic situation - Bart denying the call is for the rightful owner. Solid character mischief.

jokes.text: I just paid $5 million for the screen rights to the book Everybody Poops.
jokes.setup: Hollywood producer call
jokes.punchline: Major movie deal for children's book about defecation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Hollywood satire - absurd high-budget adaptation of simple children's book. Perfect lampooning of industry excess.

jokes.text: Only one face came to mind for the constipated gorilla: yours.
jokes.setup: Casting Denis Leary in Everybody Poops movie
jokes.punchline: Specific casting as constipated gorilla
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of absurd casting. Specific enough to be funny, backhanded compliment about Denis Leary's face.

jokes.text: I want to do my own poops.
jokes.setup: Denis Leary negotiating movie terms
jokes.punchline: Wanting to do own stunts but for defecation scenes
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of actor ego - wanting to do own 'stunts' for bathroom scenes. Excellent economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Boston comedian-turned-movie-star-turned-basic-cable-notable?
jokes.setup: Identifying Denis Leary
jokes.punchline: Describing his career trajectory as decline
jokes.explanation: Harsh but accurate career summary. Good escalating structure showing decline from movies to cable TV.

jokes.text: I'm looking for Maya. Last name Normousbutt.
jokes.setup: Prank call to Hawaiian bar
jokes.punchline: Fake name that sounds like 'my enormous butt'
jokes.explanation: Standard prank call joke. Classic format but predictable. Good for Bart's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Better put it down then, mate.
jokes.setup: Bartender holding Drew P. Wiener
jokes.punchline: Australian's literal interpretation of crude name
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to prank call. Australian character adds flavor, literal interpretation of 'holding' a name works well.

jokes.text: Olaf Myfriendsaregay.
jokes.setup: Another prank call to German bar
jokes.punchline: Name that sounds like 'all of my friends are gay'
jokes.explanation: Another prank call following same formula. Less clever than previous ones, relies on easy target.

jokes.text: I will thank you for showing me the futility of human endeavor.
jokes.setup: German responding to prank call realization
jokes.punchline: Philosophical response about existential meaninglessness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected angry reaction. German character delivers profound philosophical response to prank call. Excellent character comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, the sun never sets on the Bartish Empire.
jokes.setup: Bart celebrating his global prank calling success
jokes.punchline: Pun on British Empire with his own name
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun with Bart's name. Good character moment showing his pride in global reach of pranks.

jokes.text: Which one, the one that got canceled or the one that's gonna get canceled?
jokes.setup: Denis Leary mentioning his TV show
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal assessment of his TV career prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-humor about TV show cancellation. Brutal but accurate prediction delivered with perfect economy.

jokes.text: I'm desperate for any signs of adult approval.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining why he told on Bart
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of psychological neediness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment. Painfully honest self-awareness about his psychological needs. Dark but true to character.

jokes.text: No it's not. It's easy! I'll tell you what's hard: having to watch my stunt double run into a burning building, worrying that people might realize it's not me; it's him.
jokes.setup: Marge saying parenting is hard
jokes.punchline: Denis Leary's completely backwards priority about stunt work
jokes.explanation: Excellent celebrity satire. Denis Leary's concern about stunt doubles being discovered is perfectly backwards and narcissistic.

jokes.text: I know I said you couldn't have a cell phone, but, in these days of stranger dangers and sinister ministers...
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why Bart can have phone
jokes.punchline: Alliterative pairing of 'stranger dangers and sinister ministers'
jokes.explanation: Good Marge-style alliterative phrase. Typical overly cautious parenting reasoning with nice wordplay.

jokes.text: in these days of stranger dangers and sinister ministers,
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why Bart needs phone
jokes.punchline: Alliterative pairing of 'stranger dangers and sinister ministers'
jokes.explanation: Nice alliterative wordplay typical of Marge's earnest speaking style. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Like a big-mouthed bass.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Bart took the bait
jokes.punchline: Homer's fishing metaphor response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective fishing metaphor. Good Homer character moment showing his simple understanding of the situation.

jokes.text: That's a hard hat zone! You sure this is safe? Sure, I got my seat belt on.
jokes.setup: Concern about Bart in construction zone
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards safety priorities - seat belt vs hard hat
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic failure - focusing on irrelevant safety measure while ignoring appropriate one.

jokes.text: Uh,... trying to do something constructive?
jokes.setup: Marge asking what Bart's doing at construction site
jokes.punchline: Bart's weak pun on 'constructive' at construction site
jokes.explanation: Weak but characteristic Bart pun. Simple wordplay that fits his character's attempt to deflect trouble.

jokes.text: Hey, Bart just keeps spinning around and around and around. What's his angle?
jokes.setup: Watching Bart's GPS signal spinning in circles
jokes.punchline: Homer's unintentional pun on 'angle' while watching spinning
jokes.explanation: Good unintentional pun that works on multiple levels. Decent visual comedy element.

jokes.text: Uh... we're doing laundry. Dad, do laundry.
jokes.setup: Parents catching Bart and Milhouse in laundry room
jokes.punchline: Bart commanding Homer to prove their story by doing laundry
jokes.explanation: Clever cover story requiring Homer's participation. Good character moment showing Bart's quick thinking.

jokes.text: Soon you'll be mine. Keep talking, creep-o. Every word buys you another year in the slammer.
jokes.setup: Two characters using tracking technology for different purposes
jokes.punchline: Criminal and cop both using surveillance, cop encouraging more evidence
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure showing both criminal and police using same tracking technology. Nice character moments for both.

jokes.text: Viagra at five dollars a pill? Whatever it is, it's goin' in Skinner's coffee.
jokes.setup: Bart receiving spam text
jokes.punchline: Plan to drug principal regardless of what medication it is
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about drugging authority figure. Good character mischief but potentially problematic.

jokes.text: What is in this coffee?! Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Earlier reference to putting Viagra in Skinner's coffee
jokes.punchline: Skinner's reaction and Chalmers' knowing response
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier setup. Chalmers' knowing response suggests he's aware of the situation.

jokes.text: He must be running away! Eh, he won't get far. Not on the crappy breakfast I made for him.
jokes.setup: Tracking shows Bart moving fast
jokes.punchline: Homer's confidence based on poor breakfast quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - using his own inadequacy as parenting strategy. Good self-aware character moment.

jokes.text: First, I'm gonna take a bath. Then I'm gonna eat some vegetables and then I'm gonna get to bed nice and early, with no TV.
jokes.setup: Lisa having house to herself
jokes.punchline: Her idea of rebellion is wholesome activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment. Her rebellion consists entirely of healthy, responsible activities. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: My mom's gonna wake up soon. She gets upset if someone's not there to tell her where she is.
jokes.setup: Milhouse needing to go home
jokes.punchline: His mother's apparent cognitive issues requiring location reminders
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family dysfunction. Suggests serious mental health issues treated casually. Very Milhouse.

jokes.text: Listen Bart, if I don't get home soon, there won't be time for my mom to give me a hug, my dad to read me a story, and both of them to sing me a song until I gently fall asleep.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining his bedtime routine
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, infantilizing bedtime ritual for a 10-year-old
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse overprotection. Elaborate bedtime routine emphasizing his infantilization by overprotective parents.

jokes.text: Real boogeyman weather.
jokes.setup: Bart noting the dark, moonless night
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact assessment of scary conditions
jokes.explanation: Good Bart observation. Casual acknowledgment of scary conditions shows his fearlessness.

jokes.text: You've been flying south because you're migrating to... Machu Picchu, Peru!
jokes.setup: Bart realizes bird is migrating
jokes.punchline: Connecting to Lisa's earlier disappointment about never seeing Machu Picchu
jokes.explanation: Good plot callback connecting Lisa's dream to Bart's scheme. Sets up the family's journey.

jokes.text: Day is awesome! Night is scary. Day is awesome! Night is scary...
jokes.setup: Bart alone at night getting scared
jokes.punchline: His repetitive mantra revealing his fear
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Bart's vulnerability. Repetitive mantra effectively shows his growing anxiety.

jokes.text: That unpleasantness in Ecuador is behind us. My coca leaf addiction is under control.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to stay positive about their journey
jokes.punchline: Casual references to serious problems in South America
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about off-screen adventures. Homer's casual mention of drug addiction and international incidents.

jokes.text: Clearly the work of Wirakocha, the trickster god.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing a bush instead of Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's sudden expertise in Incan mythology
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment showing random knowledge acquisition. Attributing simple mistake to ancient deity is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Our mothers were the most overprotective in the greater Machu Picchu metropolitan area.
jokes.setup: Guide explaining ancient civilization
jokes.punchline: Using modern metropolitan area terminology for ancient city
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic humor mixing ancient and modern concepts. Solid structure.

jokes.text: Those walls do look safe. Walls that keep out danger keep out knowledge.
jokes.setup: Marge admiring protective walls of Machu Picchu
jokes.punchline: Guide's philosophical insight about overprotection
jokes.explanation: Excellent wisdom delivered with perfect economy. Profound parenting insight that drives the episode's theme.

jokes.text: I didn't mean to. I'm just chatty. I sold my business a few years ago and I miss talking to people.
jokes.setup: Guide apologizing for teaching lesson
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to explain real motivation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about character having human motivations beyond plot function.

jokes.text: I didn't mean to. I'm just chatty. I sold my business a few years ago and I miss talking to people.
jokes.setup: Guide delivering profound wisdom
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually a retired businessman who's just lonely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of wise native guide trope. Reveals profound wisdom came from random chatting, not ancient wisdom.

jokes.text: but all I see is a stupid bird with a tracking chip on its leg. It doesn't add up.
jokes.setup: Computer saying Bart is standing on computer
jokes.punchline: Homer's rare moment of logical deduction
jokes.explanation: Good irony of Homer being logical for once. Sets up resolution of tracking plot.

jokes.text: Were you guys gone? I didn't even notice.
jokes.setup: Parents return after two-week journey
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal of their absence
jokes.explanation: Good Bart coolness under pressure. His casual dismissal of their epic journey is perfectly in character.

tmdb_overview: Bart lands himself in trouble when he steals a mobile phone from Denis Leary and subsequently uses it to land the actor a comical film role. The troublemaker then leads his family to the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu after Marge confiscates the device and decides to use its GPS technology to track her son.
